---
jupyter: python3
# jupyter: peshbeen
format:
  revealjs:
    css: style.css
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    footer: "[From Admission to Discharge: Dynamically Updating Length of Stay Forecasts with Causal Diagnostic and Operational Penalties](https://mustafaslancoto.github.io/talks/presentations/qqf.html)"
    slide-number: c/t
    preview-links: auto
    # chalkboard: true
    html-math-method: mathjax
    # margin: 0.1
    # min-scale: 0.2
    # max-scale: 2.0
---

{{< include title-slide.html >}}

##

<br>
<br>

[Outline]{.mimic-h2}


- [**The Operational Problem**]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}
- [**Solution**]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}
- [**The Framework**]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}
- [**The Causal Location Shift Example**]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}
- [**Counterfactual Simulation & Impact**]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}

---



## The "Operational Problem"

:::{style="padding: 10px; margin: 10px 0; border-left: 2px solid #BF505C; text-align: left; color: #BF505C; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.2em;"}
Current hospital operations rely on aggregate comorbidity scores (e.g., Charlson), which compress complex medical histories, masking the true operational drivers of discharge delays.
:::

<br>

::::{.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
- **The Problem:** We cannot manage what we do not isolate.
- **The Gap:** Traditional models suffer from selection bias; diagnostics are endogenous.
- **Our Approach:** Move from "Correlative Scores" to "Causal Penalties."
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/gemini_congest.png)
:::
::::

---

## Solution

### Dynamic Thresholding: Real-Time Updating
[We update the survival curve ($P(LoS > threshold)$) dynamically as clinical data arrives (11 am daily).]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}

<br>

:::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;"}
::: {.column width="30%" style="background-color: #f1f5f9; padding: 20px; border-radius: 10px; border: 2px solid #cbd5e1;"}
### 1. Admission Day
* Baseline prediction based on initial exisitng data
* Initial $P(LoS > \text{threshold})$
:::

::: {.column width="5%" style="text-align: center; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"}
[**➔**]{style="font-size: 3em; color: #BF505C;"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%" style="background-color: #EFECE1; padding: 20px; border-radius: 10px; border: 2px solid #dcdac8;"}
### 2. Mid-Stay Update
* Diagnosis confirmed
* **Causal Shift:** Apply $\tau_D$ penalty to the distribution
:::

::: {.column width="5%" style="text-align: center; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"}
[**➔**]{style="font-size: 3em; color: #BF505C;"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%" style="background-color: #e5f0f2; padding: 20px; border-radius: 10px; border: 2px solid #b7d5da;"}
### 3. Action Trigger
* **Condition:** $P(LoS > \text{threshold}) \ge 55\%$
* **Action:** Refer to MDT / Community pathways
:::
::::

---

## The Framework

### 1. Purging Confounders

<br>

We utilize Double Machine Learning (DML) to isolate the causal impact of diagnostics ($\tau_D$) and ward transfers ($\tau_W$) from the "clean-path" LoS.

$$
Y_{purged} = Total\_LoS - \sum_{i=1}^{n} \tau_{D_i} -  \tau_{W_j}
$$

:::{.fragment}
:::{style="margin-top: 30px;"}
- **Identification:** DML partials out high-dimensional confounders (age, gender, ward busyness) to estimate the true "bed-day burden" of specific diagnoses.
:::
:::

:::{.fragment}
:::{style="margin-top: 20px;"}
- **Purging:** We subtract realized shocks from the observed outcome to create an idealized survival curve $S_0(t)$—the "clean-path" prediction in the absence of operational friction.
:::
:::

---

## The Framework

### 2. Modeling Uncertainty of the Diagnostic Penalty For Updating the $\hat{LoS}$

[We treat diagnostic impact not as a fixed constant, but as a distribution, accounting for clinical uncertainty.]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}

<br>

:::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;"}

::: {.column width="55%" style="padding: 20px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid #2CA0AB;" .fragment}

### The Convolution
To generate a robust "Tail Risk" LoS forecasts, we convolve the baseline LoS distribution ($f_L$) with the diagnostic uncertainty ($\tau_D \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu, \sigma^2)$):

$$
f_{new}(z) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_L(z - \tau) \cdot f_\tau(\tau) \, d\tau
$$

- **$f_L(z - \tau)$:** The baseline LoS probability.
- **$f_\tau(\tau)$:** The clinical uncertainty of the diagnostic penalty.

:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="40%" style="padding: 20px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid #2CA0AB;" .fragment}

### Why this matters for the MDT:

* **Beyond the Average:** It captures the "Tail Risk"—the patients who face both a complex diagnosis and a slow recovery.
* **Operational Buffer:** By modeling the variance of the effect ($\sigma^2$), we create a "safety margin" for discharge planning.
* **Robustness:** This prevents underestimating the probability of a patient becoming "Super-Stranded" ($\ge 14$ days).

:::

::::


---


## The Causal Location Shift Example

[The DML framework treats the diagnostic confirmation as a "shock" that shifts the distribution's location along the time axis.]{style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #1A6872;"}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.stats import lognorm

# Set theme colors to match your presentation
color_baseline = "#1A6872"
color_shifted = "#BF505C"
threshold = 14
plt.rcParams['figure.facecolor'] = '#FBFAF4'
plt.rcParams['axes.facecolor'] = '#FBFAF4'

# Generate synthetic LoS data (Log-Normal)
# Baseline: Mean LoS ~8 days
s, loc, scale = 0.5, 0, 8
x = np.linspace(0, 30, 500)
pdf_baseline = lognorm.pdf(x, s, loc, scale)

# Shifted: Diagnostic penalty adds a +4 day shift to the location
pdf_shifted = lognorm.pdf(x, s, loc + 4, scale)

# Calculate Probabilities P(LoS > 14)
prob_baseline = 1 - lognorm.cdf(threshold, s, loc, scale)
prob_shifted = 1 - lognorm.cdf(threshold, s, loc + 4, scale)

# Plotting
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 5), facecolor="#FBFAF4")
ax = plt.gca()
ax.set_facecolor("#FBFAF4")

# 1. Baseline Plot
plt.plot(x, pdf_baseline, color=color_baseline, lw=3, label=f'Day 0 Baseline (P > 14: {prob_baseline:.1%})')
plt.fill_between(x, 0, pdf_baseline, where=(x > threshold), color=color_baseline, alpha=0.2)

# 2. Shifted Plot
plt.plot(x, pdf_shifted, color=color_shifted, lw=3, linestyle="--", label=f'Updated with Diagnosis (P > 14: {prob_shifted:.1%})')
plt.fill_between(x, 0, pdf_shifted, where=(x > threshold), color=color_shifted, alpha=0.3)

# Threshold Line
plt.axvline(threshold, color="black", linestyle=":", lw=2)
plt.text(threshold+0.5, plt.ylim()[1]*0.6, f"Hospital Threshold (e.g., {threshold} days)", rotation=0, fontweight="bold", fontsize=16)

plt.title("Location-Shift Model: Impact of Diagnostic Shock on Tail Risk", fontsize=20)
plt.xlabel("Days in Hospital (LoS)", fontsize=16)
plt.ylabel("Probability Density", fontsize=16)
plt.legend(fontsize=16)
plt.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.show()
```

:::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;"}

::: {.column width="48%" style="background-color: #f1f5f9; padding: 15px; border-radius: 10px;"}
### 🟢 Day 0: Baseline
The "clean-path" prediction based on demographics and only initial information. The shaded area represents the initial risk of becoming a stranded patient.
:::

::: {.column width="4%"}
:::

::: {.column width="48%" style="background-color: #fee2e2; padding: 15px; border-radius: 10px;"}
### 🔴 Day $k$: Causal Update
Diagnosis $\tau_D$ shifts the entire distribution right. The shaded area shows sn increase in the probability of crossing the 14-day threshold.
:::

::::

---


## Counterfactual Simulation & Impact

<br>

To prove the framework's worth, we replay the historical baseline as a simulation:

:::{.fragment}
- **Step 1:** Run a day-by-day replay of historical patients.
- **Step 2:** Apply the policy trigger (the "Model Intervention") the moment a patient hits the risk threshold.
- **Step 3:** Compute the System Delta ($\Delta Beds$):

$$
\Delta \text{Beds} = \sum \text{Actual LoS}_i - \sum \text{Counterfactual LoS}_i
$$


:::

:::{.fragment}
:::{style="padding: 10px; margin: 10px 0; border-left: 2px solid #2CA0AB; text-align: left; color: #2CA0AB; font-weight: bold;"}
This allows us to present decision-makers with a concrete metric: "If we had used this model, we would have reclaimed X bed-days."
:::
:::

# Any questions or thoughts? 💬

![](images/follow_us.png){fig-align="center"}
